title: Bridge Essay: Drug Addiction: Updating Actions to Habits to Compulsions Ten Years On.
concept: Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Drug Addiction: Updating Actions to Habits to Compulsions Ten Years On.** — Annu Rev Psychol (2016). DOI: 10.1146/annurev-psych-122414-033457. PMID: 26253543. A decade ago, we hypothesized that drug addiction can be viewed as a transition from voluntary, recreational drug use to compulsive drug-seeking habits, neurally underpinned by a transition from prefrontal cortical to striatal control over drug seeking and taking as well as a progression from the ventral to the dorsal striatum.
